old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-i2r0","title":"ForwardingBuffer: _source_order can accumulate duplicates","description":"File: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:317-321

The _touch_source method uses list.remove() which only removes the FIRST occurrence:

```python
def _touch_source(self, source_iid: bytes) -> None:
    if source_iid in self._source_order:
        self._source_order.remove(source_iid)  # removes first occurrence only
    self._source_order.append(source_iid)
```

WHY IT'S A BUG: If _source_order somehow gets corrupted with duplicates (e.g., through a bug in concurrent access), remove() will only clean up one occurrence. Subsequent appends will grow the list with more duplicates. The 'in' check uses list linear search, and remove() is also O(n), so duplicates accumulate quadratic slowdown.

Similarly, line 245 and 283 call `_source_order.remove(source_iid)` without handling duplicates.

IMPACT: Memory growth and O(n^2) performance degradation if duplicates accumulate. The LRU eviction (line 212) pops index 0, so duplicate IIDs would cause eviction of the wrong source.

FIX: Either use a set for O(1) membership, or add assertions/guards that duplicates cannot exist. Consider using collections.OrderedDict for the source ordering similar to GradientTable.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:22.223208Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:22.223245Z"}

Consider using collections.OrderedDict for the source ordering similar to GradientTable.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:22.223208Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-23T03:44:11.007627645Z","started_at":"2026-07-23T03:44:11.007627645Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-23T03:49:11.007627645Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-23T03:44:11.007627645Z"}
new_value: {"notes":"Using OrderedDict like GradientTable to prevent duplicate accumulation in _source_order (per explore subagent analysis)"}
